Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \sin^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \sin^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 3, \quad y = 4, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{3}{5}\right)}{\frac{4}{5}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1}{2}

Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \tan^{-1}\left(-\frac{4}{3}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \tan^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 3, \quad y = -4, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{4}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = -\sqrt{\frac{1 - \left(\frac{3}{5}\right)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = -\frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(-\frac{15}{17}\right)}{\frac{8}{17}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = 4

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 15, \quad y = 8, \quad r = 17

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{8}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{15}{17}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{15}{17}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}

\text{Based on the domain of } \cot^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } II.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = -7, \quad y = 24, \quad r = 25

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{24}{25}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{7}{25}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(-\frac{7}{25}\right)}{\frac{24}{25}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{4}{3}

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 3, \quad y = 4, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 - \left(\frac{3}{5}\right)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(-\frac{7}{25}\right)}{\frac{24}{25}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{4}{3}

\text{Based on the domain of } \tan^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 7, \quad y = -24, \quad r = 25

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{24}{25}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{7}{25}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{7}{25}\right)}{-\frac{24}{25}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = -\frac{3}{4}

\text{Based on the domain of } \csc^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 12, \quad y = 5, \quad r = 13

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{5}{13}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{12}{13}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{12}{13}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{5 \, \sqrt{26}}{26}

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 3, \quad y = 4, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{3}{5}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{2 \, \sqrt{5}}{5}

\text{Based on the domain of } \sin^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 5, \quad y = -12, \quad r = 13

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{12}{13}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{5}{13}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{5}{13}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{3 \, \sqrt{13}}{13}

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 12, \quad y = 5, \quad r = 13

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{5}{13}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{12}{13}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{12}{13}\right)}{\frac{5}{13}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1}{5}

\text{Based on the domain of } \sin^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 5, \quad y = -12, \quad r = 13

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{12}{13}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{5}{13}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{5}{13}\right)}{-\frac{12}{13}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = -\frac{2}{3}

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{24}{25}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{7}{25}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(-\frac{7}{25}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{3}{5}

\text{Based on the domain of } \cos^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 4, \quad y = 3, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{4}{5}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{3 \, \sqrt{10}}{10}

\text{Based on the domain of } \cot^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } II.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = -15, \quad y = 8, \quad r = 17

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{8}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{15}{17}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(-\frac{15}{17}\right)}{\frac{8}{17}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = 4

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{15}{17}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{17}}{17}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{4}{5}\right)}{\frac{3}{5}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1}{3}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{4}{5}\right)}{2}}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{8}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{15}{17}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{15}{17}\right)}{\frac{8}{17}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1}{4}

\text{Based on the domain of } \sec^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } II.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = -12, \quad y = 5, \quad r = 13

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{5}{13}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{12}{13}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(-\frac{12}{13}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{26}}{26}

\text{Based on the domain of } \tan^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 24, \quad y = 7, \quad r = 25

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{7}{25}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{24}{25}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 - \left(\frac{24}{25}\right)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{10}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{24}{25}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{7}{25}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{7}{25}\right)}{\frac{24}{25}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{3}{4}

\text{Based on the domain of } \csc^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 7, \quad y = -24, \quad r = 25

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{24}{25}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{7}{25}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = -\sqrt{\frac{1 - \left(\frac{7}{25}\right)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = -\frac{3}{5}

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 8, \quad y = 15, \quad r = 17

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{15}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{8}{17}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{8}{17}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{5 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}

\text{Based on the domain of } \cos^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } II.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = -24, \quad y = 7, \quad r = 25

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{7}{25}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{24}{25}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 - \left(-\frac{24}{25}\right)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{7 \, \sqrt{2}}{10}

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 3, \quad y = 4, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{3}{5}\right)}{\frac{4}{5}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1}{2}

\text{Based on the domain of } \cos^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 4, \quad y = 3, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{4}{5}\right)}{\frac{3}{5}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1}{3}

\text{Based on the domain of } \tan^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 3, \quad y = 4, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{3}{5}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{2 \, \sqrt{5}}{5}

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = -3, \quad y = 4, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{3}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 - \left(-\frac{3}{5}\right)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{2 \, \sqrt{5}}{5}

\text{Based on the domain of } \tan^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 8, \quad y = 15, \quad r = 17

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{15}{17}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{8}{17}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{8}{17}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{5 \, \sqrt{34}}{34}

\text{Based on the domain of } \csc^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 7, \quad y = 24, \quad r = 25

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{24}{25}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{7}{25}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 - \left(\frac{7}{25}\right)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{3}{5}
